content:
✅ Why This Works
• Every fact or writing insight immediately becomes a discussion point.
• Ensures writing lessons feel interactive rather than passive.
• Expands critical thinking beyond just literature.

📜 3. Referencing Rule: Direct Quote & Source for Every Literary Reference

📜 Every literary mention now includes:
✔ The source (novel, poem, essay, etc.).
✔ A direct quote to contextualize the reference.

Example: Week 10 – Dystopian Literature & The State’s Control of Narrative

📜 “Big Brother’s omnipresent gaze controls Winston in 1984, shaping his perception of truth. Orwell describes it: ‘We do not merely destroy our enemies; we change them.’”

📢 Discussion Question: Can control over language change thought? Where do we see this in modern media?
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 142 (chunk 142/555)
